analysis.name: Alberto Botter
analysis.verdict: Tough course, but students consistently rate the teaching highly, and ratings have been declining

review_highlights.comment: Must take! An extremely well paced class with clarity. Alberto seeks feedbacks after each lecture and adjusts based on them. He makes sure everyone truly understands the concepts and how they apply in practice. You will get unique practitioners' views and genuine recruiting advice from him and guest lecturers that you won't get elsewhere.

The course is well-structured, balancing theory and practical applications. The instructor keeps students motivated, encourages participation, and is receptive to feedback. Real-world insights from speakers, group projects, and case studies enhance learning, while support for recruiting adds further value.

Pros: He gives basic insight to his work at AQR. He is engaging in the first few classes. Tries his best to make many points clear to students.

Cons: HW was a bit much, you have to use AI for it because there's a gap between the theory and application. His grading is very suspect. He does not make things clear.

Got a good grade but worked for it.

Professor Botter is an exceptional professor. He actively seeks feedback about his teaching and class structure and impressively improves upon it weekly. What stands out the most is his professionalism, his engaging and well-organized lectures, and his genuine commitment to student success. I feel truly lucky to have had him as a professor.

My team and I were able to discuss our research and strategy frameworks during interviews for quant roles in the bank and Hedge Funds. Professor Botter's class is widely regarded as one of the popular and most impactful courses at Columbia University, within the Mathematical Finance program.

Rising intellectual tides support each student’s float to scholar status by course end. Great!

Of all the teaching qualities that Professor Buhle possesses his eagerness for students to learn the material he presents is seen in the multiple methods of learning and tutoring that he employees in lectures. Professor Buhle is a wonderful professor. He challenges his students, but is always there to explain and help. Jason is a great and I recommend any class he teaches.

review_highlights.comment: Prof Altshiler makes hard material much easier. Prof. Altshiler's class is life changing.  I was scared of public speaking and never believed that I could handle a trial.  I went from hesitantly participating in class to handling a full cross examination at trial in federal court.  It was an amazing experience and I feel very confident as a result.  I never imagined myself really doing all this

I recently saw Prof Altshiler on TV.  I thought to myself, that's right, I had her as my professor.  She really is every bit as amazing for every minute of a two hour lecture.  So many professors are dull and boring.  Prof Altshiler is nothing like that.  I wish more professors were like her.  She makes class interesting and exciting and real.

You can tell she cares. She is super passionate about trials and her excitement and passion make you want to participate and get excited about the topic too. Unlike some dry professors, she engages everyone and encourages involvement. I've never felt so enthusiastic about a course as I did in this class. Why isn't she offering more courses?
